0.phonetic: /ɔːl aɪ ˈevər ˈwɑːntɪd wəz fɔːr ˈdʒuːli ˈbeɪkər tu liːv mi əˈloʊn/

keyPoints.explanation: 常用主语结构，指我长久以来唯一的心愿；例句：All I ever wanted was a simple life.（我一直以来唯一想要的就是简单的生活）

keyPoints.expression: was for sb to do sth
keyPoints.explanation: 用于表达内心期盼，愿望是让某人做某事；例句：My hope was for him to tell me the truth.（我的期盼是他能对我说实话）
口语固定短语，不要打扰某人、别缠着某人；例句：Please leave me alone when I study.（我学习的时候请别打扰我）

alts.verb: The only wish I had was that Juli Baker wouldn’t bother me.
alts.meaning: 我唯一的心愿就是朱莉·贝克不要来打扰我

alts.verb: I just hoped Juli Baker would keep away from me.
alts.meaning: 我只希望朱莉·贝克离我远一些

0.context: All I ever wanted was for my little brother to leave me alone while I read books. 看书的时候，我唯一的心愿就是弟弟别来烦我。

1.cn: 一切都要从1957年二年级开学前的那个夏天说起。
1.en: It all began in the summer of 1957 right before second grade started.

alts.verb: Everything started the summer of 1957 ahead of the second grade.
alts.meaning: 所有事情都发生在1957年二年级开学前的夏天。

alts.verb: The whole story took place in the summer of 1957 prior to second grade.
alts.meaning: 整件事发生在1957年二年级之前的夏天。

1.phonetic: /ɪt ɔːl bɪˈɡæn ɪn ðə ˈsʌmər əv ˈnaɪnˈfɪftiˈsevn raɪt bɪˈfɔːr ˈsekənd ɡreɪd ˈstɑːrtɪd/

keyPoints.explanation: 叙事开篇固定句式，意为一切始于……，常用于回忆往事；例句：It all began on a rainy winter morning.（一切都始于一个下雨的冬日清晨。）
副词短语，就在……之前，加强时间紧迫感；例句：I arrived right before the class began.（我刚好在上课前赶到。）

1.context: It all began in the summer of 2024 right before my senior year started. 一切都始于2024年高三开学前的那个夏天。

2.en: before the start of second grade.

examples: Prior to the commencement of second grade.
Right before second grade started.
Just before I started second grade.
Before second grade.

4.cn: "你们觉得这地方怎么样？"一人开口问道，另一人说这里很棒，紧接着就问自己的房间是什么颜色。
4.en: "What do you guys think of this place?" one asked, another replied that it was cool before asking what color his room would be.

alts.verb: "How do you like this house?" someone questioned, while another said it looked nice and wondered about his bedroom's color.
alts.meaning: "你们觉得这房子怎么样？"有人发问，另一个人表示房子很不错，还好奇自己卧室的颜色。

4.phonetic: /wʌt du ju ɡaɪz θɪŋk əv ðɪs pleɪs , wʌn æskt , əˈnʌðər rɪˈplaɪd ðæt ɪt wəz kuːl bɪˈfɔːr ˈæskɪŋ wʌt ˈkʌlər hɪz ruːm wʊd bi/

keyPoints.explanation: 美式口语常用称呼，可指代男女一群人，意为你们；例句：You guys can join us for dinner.（你们可以和我们一起吃晚饭。）
固定搭配，认为某物怎么样，用于征求看法；例句：What do you think of the new movie?（你觉得这部新电影怎么样？）

4.context: "What do you guys think of the new café?" she asked, her friend said it was nice then asked what flavor milk tea they sold. "你们觉得这家新开的咖啡馆怎么样？"她问道，朋友说很不错，接着询问店里有什么口味的奶茶。

5.cn: -看起来不错 棒极了  -我的房间是什么颜色
5.en: - It's cool. - Uh, what color is my room?

examples: It's satisfactory. May I know the color of my room?
It's cool. So what color is my room?
Cool place. What color's my room gonna be?
Cool. My room color?

8.cn: 不如你和我一起去帮忙卸货车上的东西，这边女士们可以去厨房着手整理布置。
8.en: Why don't you and I go help unload the van while the womenfolk head into the kitchen to start setting things up?

alts.verb: Let’s you and me assist with unloading the van, and all the ladies can go to the kitchen to begin preparing everything.
alts.meaning: 咱们俩去帮忙卸货车，各位女士可以去厨房开始筹备一切。

8.phonetic: /waɪ doʊnt ju ænd aɪ ɡoʊ help ʌnˈloʊd ðə væn waɪl ðə ˈwɪmɪnfoʊk hed ˈɪntu ðə ˈkɪtʃɪn tu stɑːrt ˈsetɪŋ θɪŋz ʌp/

keyPoints.explanation: 口语提建议经典句型，意为不如你……；例句：Why don't we take a walk outside?（我们不如出去散散步吧？）
固定搭配，卸下货车里的货物/行李；例句：We need to unload the van before dark.（我们得天黑前卸完货车。）
名词，旧时口语，妇女们、女眷；例句：The womenfolk prepared all the festival food.（女眷们准备了所有节庆食物。）
短语动词，布置、整理、筹备；例句：We spent an hour setting up the dining table.（我们花一小时布置餐桌。）

8.context: Why don't you and I go help unload the truck while the womenfolk head into the dining room to start setting up for the party? 不如你我一起去卸卡车，女眷们可以去餐厅布置派对场地。

9.cn: 女士们可以去厨房收拾一下 顺便准备午饭
9.en: ...and the womenfolk here can get in the kitchen and start setting up.

examples: And the ladies can get the kitchen going.
The women can head to the kitchen and start on lunch.
And the ladies may prepare the kitchen for lunch.
Kitchen's on you, ladies.

keyPoints.explanation: 口语通用应答词，表示好的、没问题，用于回应他人吩咐或提议；例句：Okay, I’ll finish my homework right now.（好的，我现在就写完作业。）
美式口语对父亲的亲昵称呼，爸爸；例句：Dad, can you play ball with me?（爸爸，你能陪我打球吗？）

10.context: "Go wash your hands before dinner." "Okay, Dad." “晚饭前先去洗手。”“好的，爸爸。”

11.cn: 对我来说，这便是长达五年多那段时光的开端 - 充满刻意回避与人交往的局促不安。
11.en: For me, it was the beginning of what would be more than half a decade - of strategic avoidance and social discomfort.

alts.verb: To me, this marked the start of over five years spent deliberately shying away and feeling awkward around people.
alts.meaning: 于我而言，这开启了五年多刻意躲避、与人相处倍感尴尬的日子。

11.phonetic: /fɔːr mi , ɪt wəz ðə bɪˈɡɪnɪŋ əv wʌt wʊd bi mɔːr ðæn hæf ə ˈdekɪd - əv strəˈtiːdʒɪk əˈvɔɪdəns ænd ˈsoʊʃl dɪsˈkʌmfərt/

keyPoints.explanation: 固定短语，五年；例句：I have lived here for half a decade.（我已经在这儿住五年了。）
刻意回避、有意躲开；例句：His strategic avoidance made everyone notice him.（他刻意躲闪的样子反倒让所有人注意到了他。）
社交上的尴尬、与人相处的不适感；例句：He often feels social discomfort at parties.（他在派对上常会感到社交局促。）

11.context: For her, it was the beginning of what would be half a decade - of constant worry and inner unease. 对她而言，这便是五年岁月的开端 - 整日忧心忡忡、内心始终惴惴不安。

12.en: of strategic avoidance and social discomfort.

examples: Consisting of deliberate evasion and social unease.
Five years of avoiding her and feeling awkward.
Years of trying to dodge her and feeling uncomfortable.
Avoiding her for years.

18.phonetic: /oʊ noʊ maɪ mɑːm noʊz wer aɪ æm ʃi sed ɪts faɪn/

keyPoints.explanation: 知道某人的位置，know后接宾语从句；例句：My teacher knows where we are.（老师知道我们在哪里。）
口语常用语，此处表示准许、没问题；例句：You can go out, it's fine.（你可以出去，没问题。）

alts.verb: Don't worry. My mom keeps track of me and she agreed.
alts.meaning: 别担心，妈妈清楚我的行踪，她也同意了。

alts.verb: No worries. Mom knows my whereabouts and said I could stay.
alts.meaning: 放心吧，妈妈知道我在哪，还允许我留下来。

18.context: Oh, no, my mom knows where I am. She said it's fine to play outside for a while. 不会的，我从不乱跑，我很乖，妈妈也允许我在外面玩一会儿。

27.cn: 我正打算让她离我远点，怪事却发生了。 - 我简直不敢相信。 - 我就那样和这个陌生女孩手拉着手站在原地。
27.en: I was about to tell her to get lost when the weirdest thing happened. - I couldn't believe it. - There I was holding hands with this strange girl.

alts.verb: I was going to ask her to go away when something really odd took place. - I was totally shocked. - I found myself holding hands with this unfamiliar girl.
alts.meaning: 我正要让她走开，一件十分古怪的事发生了。 - 我完全惊呆了。 - 我竟和这个陌生的女孩牵起了手。

27.phonetic: /aɪ wəz əˈbaʊt tu tel hɚ tu ɡet lɔːst wen ðə ˈwɪrdɪst θɪŋ ˈhæpənd . - aɪ ˈkʊdnt bɪˈliːv ɪt . - ðer aɪ wəz ˈhoʊldɪŋ hændz wɪð ðɪs streɪndʒ ɡɜːrl ./

keyPoints.explanation: 固定句型，意为正要做某事；例句：I was about to leave when the phone rang.（我正要离开，电话就响了。）
口语表达，让某人走开、别来打扰；例句：Leave me alone and get lost!（别烦我，走开！）
固定搭配，牵手、手拉手；例句：They walked along the street holding hands.（他们手牵手走在街上。）

27.context: I was about to head home when a sudden rain came. - I couldn't believe it. - There I was standing in the rain with my new friend. 我正准备回家，突然下起了雨。 - 我简直不敢相信。 - 我就那样和新朋友一同站在雨中。

examples: I just couldn't believe it.
Honestly, I couldn't believe my eyes.
I found it utterly unbelievable.
Unbelievable.

29.cn: 会拉着一个陌生女孩儿的手 傻站在那儿
29.en: There I was holding hands with this strange girl.

examples: There I stood, holding the hand of a girl I had never met before.
There I was — holding hands with this girl I didn't even know.
I was standing there, holding hands with some random girl.
Holding hands with a stranger.

keyPoints.explanation: 口语常用短句，表达惊讶发现对方在场，意为你在这儿、你也来了；例句：Lily? You're here too.（莉莉？你也来了。）

alts.verb: Bryce? I didn't expect to see you here.
alts.meaning: 布莱斯？没想到能在这儿碰到你。

36.context: Leo? You're here. 里奥？你也在这儿。

37.cn: 一切都明摆着了： - 学校再也不会是我的避风港。
37.en: it was clear: - School would not be a sanctuary.

alts.verb: It was obvious: - I couldn't escape trouble even at school.
alts.meaning: 事实一目了然： - 就算在学校我也躲不开麻烦。

37.phonetic: /ɪt wəz klɪr - skuːl wʊd nɑːt bi ə ˈsæŋktʃueri/

keyPoints.explanation: 常用句式，意思是显而易见、一切都清楚了；例句：It was clear that he didn't want to join us.（很明显他不想和我们一起。）
名词，避难所、能让人安心躲避烦恼的地方；例句：My bedroom is my sanctuary after a long day.（忙碌一天后，卧室是我的避风港。）

37.context: it was clear: - My bedroom would not be a sanctuary from noisy family members. 一切都显而易见： - 我的卧室也无法成为躲开吵闹家人的避风港。

38.cn: 上学都摆脱不了她的魔爪
38.en: School would not be a sanctuary.

examples: School wouldn't be a safe place either.
Even school wasn't going to be my escape.
No escape here.
School would not serve as a place of refuge.

42.cn: 布莱斯和朱莉同坐在树上 - 亲、亲、吻、吻
42.en: Bryce and Juli sitting in a tree - K-I-S-S-I-N-G

alts.verb: Bryce and Juli are perched on a tree - Sharing a sweet kiss
alts.meaning: 布莱斯和朱莉栖坐在树上 - 甜蜜相吻

42.phonetic: /braɪs ænd ˈdʒuːli ˈsɪtɪŋ ɪn ə triː - kɪsɪŋ/

keyPoints.explanation: 坐在树上；例句：The kids are sitting in a tree and chatting happily.（孩子们坐在树上开心地聊着天。）
动词kiss的现在分词，意为亲吻；此处为孩童调侃歌谣里的趣味拼写形式；例句：They were laughing and kissing.（他们笑着亲吻彼此。）

42.context: Tom and Lily sitting in a tree - H-U-G-G-I-N-G. 汤姆和莉莉同坐在树上 - 拥、抱、相、偎。

examples: K-I-S-S-I-N-G.

44.cn: 我在镇上的第一年简直一团糟。 - 你看看他们。 - 接下来三年也好不到哪去。
44.en: My first year in town was a disaster. - Look at them. - And the next three weren't much better.

alts.verb: The first year I lived here was terrible. - Just watch them. - The following three years were barely any improvement.
alts.meaning: 我住在这里的第一年十分难熬。 - 瞧瞧他们。 - 之后三年几乎没有半点好转。

44.phonetic: /maɪ fɜːrst jɪr ɪn taʊn wəz ə dɪˈzæstər - lʊk æt ðəm - ænd ðə nekst θri wɜːrnt mʌtʃ ˈbetər/

keyPoints.explanation: 口语常用，形容事情一塌糊涂、糟糕透顶；例句：My trip yesterday was a disaster.（我昨天的出行简直糟透了。）
固定表达，也好不了多少、没好到哪里去；例句：The second exam wasn't much better than the first.（第二次考试比第一次也好不到哪去。）

44.context: My first week at the new job was a disaster. - Look at my messy desk. - And the next two weeks weren't much better. 我新工作的第一周简直糟透了。 - 看看我乱糟糟的办公桌。 - 接下来两周也好不到哪去。

examples: Just look at them.
Look at those two.
Look.
Observe them.

46.en: And the next three weren't much better.

examples: And things didn't improve much for the next three years either.
And the subsequent three years showed little improvement.
And the next three years weren't much better.
Next three: same.

47.cn: 但终于到六年级时，我决定采取行动。 - 我策划好了一套方案。
47.en: But finally, in the sixth grade, I took action. - I hatched the plan.

alts.verb: Yet at last when I was in Grade Six, I decided to do something. - I worked out a scheme.
alts.meaning: 可等到上六年级，我终于决定做点什么。 - 我想出了一套对策。

47.phonetic: /bʌt ˈfaɪnəli ɪn ðə sɪkst ɡreɪd aɪ tʊk ˈækʃn - aɪ hætʃt ðə plæn/

keyPoints.explanation: 固定短语，采取行动、付诸行动；例句：If you have a problem, you need to take action right away.（遇到问题你要立刻采取行动。）
固定搭配，hatch本义孵化，引申为暗中策划、密谋计划；例句：They hatched the plan to travel abroad secretly.（他们悄悄策划了出国旅行的计划。）

47.context: But finally, after months of hesitation, I took action. - I hatched the plan to ask my parents for a trip. 犹豫数月后，我终于决定行动起来。 - 我盘算好了怎么跟爸妈提出出游的想法。

examples: I put a plan together.
I devised a strategy.
I came up with a plan.
Made a plan.

keyPoints.explanation: 固定短语，邀约（异性）外出、约会；例句：He finally plucked up courage to ask her out.（他终于鼓起勇气约她约会。）

alts.verb: I invited Sherry Stalls to go out with me.
alts.meaning: 我邀请雪莉·斯托尔斯和我一同外出。

alts.verb: I made a date with Sherry Stalls.
alts.meaning: 我和雪莉·斯托尔斯约了会。

52.context: He worked up his nerve and asked out his classmate. 他鼓起勇气邀约了同班同学约会。

54.phonetic: /tu ˈfʊli əˈpriːʃieɪt ðə ˈbrɪliəns əv ðɪs plæn - ju hæv tu ˌʌndərˈstænd ðæt ˈdʒuːli ˈheɪtɪd ˈʃeri stɔːlz/

keyPoints.explanation: 动词，此处意为领会、欣赏、意识到；例句：It's hard to appreciate his wisdom at a young age.（年少时很难领会他的智慧。）
名词，高明、精妙、出色；例句：Everyone admired the brilliance of his idea.（所有人都佩服他想法的巧妙。）

alts.verb: To truly see how clever this plan was... - ...you need to know Juli disliked Sherry Stalls very much...
alts.meaning: 想要真正看出这个计划有多巧妙…… - ……你要清楚朱莉十分反感雪莉·斯托尔斯……

54.context: To fully appreciate the fun of this game... - ...you have to understand its basic rules... 想要充分体会这款游戏的乐趣…… - ……你得先了解它的基本规则……
54.cn: 保持分段的中文，用 - 连接，例如：译文1 - 译文2 - 译文3

55.cn: 是在于 雪莉和朱莉是死对头
55.en: ...you have to understand that Juli hated Sherry Stalls...

examples: You must understand that Juli detested Sherry Stalls.
You've got to understand — Juli couldn't stand Sherry Stalls.
You have to know that Juli absolutely hated Sherry.
Juli hated Sherry.
